job_title: Senior Manager, Data Scientist - MS CLIP Valuations
strengths: Hands-on ETL and data pipeline experience
Visualization and dashboard development (Power BI, Tableau)
weaknesses: Limited explicit machine learning model experience
Shorter years of large-scale Python/ML experience
No clear model deployment or MLOps skills
improvement_bullets: Engineered and deployed machine learning classification models to predict credit risk, utilizing Scikit-Learn to automate the assessment of loan portfolio default probabilities.
Architected and deployed cloud-based data pipelines on AWS (S3/EC2), managing the full lifecycle of large-scale datasets from ingestion to ML model inference.
Led cross-functional project pods, providing technical mentorship to junior analysts and delegating tasks to ensure efficient execution of complex data modeling sprints.
Performed large-scale feature engineering on 2M+ row datasets to train predictive models, significantly enhancing the accuracy of loan default risk identification.
Developed robust MLOps workflows to automate model retraining and validation, ensuring high-performance standards for real-time credit decisioning tools.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Senior Manager, Data Scientist - MS CLIP Valuations opportunity with Capital One. I am interested in this opportunity because of Capital One's pioneering legacy in leveraging statistical modeling to disrupt the credit industry, a passion I share through my own work in data-driven decision-making. What interests me about working for Capital One is its commitment to using technology to help everyday people manage their financial lives, which intrigues me because of my own background in building predictive models to solve complex credit risk problems. I believe my expertise in Python-based data architecture and my experience in credit risk modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating complex business requirements into high-performance technical solutions. In fact, while working on my Lending Risk Portfolio Analysis project, I managed a 2M+ row dataset to identify borrower characteristics associated with default risk, creating an end-to-end ETL pipeline and performing exploratory data analysis to segment high-risk populations. This resulted in a robust model that directly informs credit decisioning frameworks. Ultimately, I know that the Senior Manager, Data Scientist - MS CLIP Valuations role at Capital One would allow me to bring my technical expertise in machine learning to the critical task of managing exposure-granting programs. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my leadership skills. In this capacity, I led a cross-functional workflow overhaul, documenting SOPs that reduced onboarding time by 50% and mentoring team members to ensure data consistency, a process that directly increased new customer acquisition by 300%. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python and SQL in working on the Mainstreet CLIP platform at Capital One. I know that my analytical rigor and my passion for scalable data solutions align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
